title: How to connect AirPods to your laptop
author: Electriz TechTalk
excerpt: AirPods aren't just for listening to tunes on your iPhone or catching up on podcasts during your morning walk - they're just as handy when you want...
content:
AirPods aren't just for listening to tunes on your iPhone or catching up on podcasts during your morning walk - they're just as handy when you want to connect them to your laptop.

Connecting your AirPods to a laptop is quick and easy. With just a few clicks you'll be able to dive into a Zoom meeting, watch a movie, or just enjoy some music without disturbing the whole room. And don't forget, your AirPods can pair with non-Apple devices too.

## A step-by-step guide to pairing AirPods

1. **Open your laptop's Bluetooth settings.** On a MacBook, go to System Preferences > Bluetooth. On Windows, click Start Menu, type 'Bluetooth', then click Bluetooth & other devices settings.
2. **Put your AirPods in pairing mode.** Make sure they're in the case with the lid open. Press and hold the button on the back until you see a flashing white light.
3. **Pair the AirPods with your laptop.** On a Mac, click Connect under Devices. On Windows, click Add Bluetooth or other device, choose Bluetooth, and select your AirPods.

## How to disconnect and reconnect AirPods

**To disconnect:** On Mac, go to the Bluetooth menu and click Disconnect. On Windows, find your AirPods in Bluetooth settings and click Disconnect.

**To reconnect:** On Mac, click Connect next to your AirPods. On Windows, go to Bluetooth settings and click Connect. Your AirPods are smart enough to reconnect automatically when they're the closest device.

## Removing AirPods from Bluetooth

On Mac: System Preferences > Bluetooth, right-click your AirPods, select Remove. On Windows: Bluetooth & other devices settings, find AirPods, select Remove device.

## Troubleshooting common issues

**AirPods won't connect**: Make sure Bluetooth is on and AirPods are charged. Try resetting by holding the back button until the light flashes amber then white.

**Sound only from one AirPod**: Put both back in the case briefly. Try cleaning the problematic one. Check audio balance settings.

**Choppy audio**: Move closer to your laptop or turn off other Bluetooth devices. Try unpairing and pairing again.

## Handy Bluetooth shortcuts for Windows

1. **Windows Key + A** opens the Action Centre for quick Bluetooth toggle.
2. **Windows Key + I** opens Settings directly.
3. **Quick Settings Menu** -- right-click the Bluetooth icon for direct access.

## Using AirPods with non-Apple devices

AirPods work with Windows laptops, Android phones, and smart TVs. Put them in pairing mode, open Bluetooth settings, and select them. Some features like auto-pause may not work on non-Apple devices.
